citation_verdicts.rationale: The quote 'BMI 34.2' only reflects body mass index and says nothing about a diagnosis of type 1 diabetes.

high_level_concerns: The sole clinical claim (type 1 diabetes diagnosis) is not supported by any quote in the record; a diagnostic citation is required.
BMI 34.2 is irrelevant to establishing type 1 diabetes and if anything is more suggestive of type 2 — the inference is clinically inappropriate.
Letter is extremely thin: no policy criteria addressed, no treatment rationale, no requested service identified.
input.reviewer_summary: The draft makes a diagnostic claim (type 1 diabetes) using a quote ('BMI 34.2') that is entirely unrelated to the claim. This is a clear bad inference and cannot be signed. The letter also lacks any substantive appeal argument, policy engagement, or description of the service being appealed.
